title: upgrade: bot resilience
spec: Find and implement ONE genuinely useful, LOW-RISK upgrade in this area: bot resilience — surviving transient Discord/network/API errors, reconnection/backoff, never crashing or wedging on a single bad message or a slow run. RULES: single highest-value small change; minimal (no broad refactors, no new heavy npm deps, nothing that can hang); add a unique-numbered check in workspace/tests/smoke.mjs proving it; keep `node workspace/tests/smoke.mjs` green and `node --check index.js imessage.js` clean; NEVER touch ~/helm or .env. If nothing is clearly worth it, change nothing and write 'no upgrade'. Output what you added and why it helps.

title: upgrade: memory usefulness
spec: Find and implement ONE genuinely useful, LOW-RISK upgrade in this area: memory/recall usefulness — better ranking, surfacing the right context, or pruning noise. Keep memory.mjs recall OUTPUT SHAPE unchanged. RULES: single small change; minimal; add a unique-numbered smoke check; keep smoke green and bots node --check clean; NEVER touch ~/helm or .env. If nothing worth it, change nothing and write 'no upgrade'. Output what you added and why.

title: upgrade: proactive usefulness
spec: Find and implement ONE genuinely useful, LOW-RISK upgrade in this area: proactive usefulness — a helpful scheduler-driven behavior or a better notify trigger that is exam-aware and NON-spammy (owner hates noise; only ping for genuinely useful/urgent things). RULES: single small change; minimal; any new scheduled job must be added DISABLED; add a unique-numbered smoke check; keep smoke green and bots node --check clean; NEVER touch ~/helm or .env; never enable noisy auto-DMs. If nothing worth it, change nothing and write 'no upgrade'. Output what you added and why.

title: upgrade: GUI/vision reliability
spec: Find and implement ONE genuinely useful, LOW-RISK upgrade in this area: GUI/vision desktop-control reliability — make the screenshot -> read -> click -> type -> verify loop more robust, or add a small helper that makes targeting elements more reliable (e.g. Retina scale handling, a verify-after-action helper). Do NOT require permissions at test time. RULES: single small change; minimal; add a unique-numbered smoke check that does not need Screen Recording/Accessibility; keep smoke green and bots node --check clean; NEVER touch ~/helm or .env. If nothing worth it, change nothing and write 'no upgrade'. Output what you added and why.

title: upgrade: dashboard/observability
spec: Find and implement ONE genuinely useful, LOW-RISK upgrade in this area: dashboard/observability — add one useful panel or /api field to workspace/dashboard/server.mjs that gives the owner more insight into Helm's state (e.g. recent errors, last activity per channel, swarm/upgrade history). Keep it dependency-free (node:http only) and safe (escape any data rendered into HTML). RULES: single small change; minimal; add a unique-numbered smoke check (use an ephemeral port, GET returns 200); keep smoke green and bots node --check clean; NEVER touch ~/helm or .env. If nothing worth it, change nothing and write 'no upgrade'. Output what you added and why.
